context.usage: Bottom floor area; can tint/texture change after cleaning.

dirty_state_notes: Dirty state includes 8-12 scattered #AB5236 pixels; do not create 2x2 blocks.
Keep the top edge of the strip (y=104) clean with no dirt pixels in the first 2 rows.

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 256x24px (2 states × 128x24) with 0px spacing.
Clean state uses only #FFA300 and #FFF1E8; Dirty state adds #AB5236 specks only.
Both states tile seamlessly from x=0 to x=127 (left and right edges match).

reference_images.label: cage_object_doc
reference_images.purpose: Cage layout, perch, bowl placement, and floor bounds anchor.

reference_images.label: art_direction_doc
reference_images.purpose: Pixel-art palette, silhouette, and warm interior mood anchor.

reference_images.label: time_system_doc
reference_images.purpose: Sleep cycle, food decay, and poop spawn cadence anchor.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle for world asset loading with nearest-neighbor sampling and deterministic z-order.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/cage_floor_clean_dirty_2f.png through AssetServer and store the handle under cage_floor_clean_dirty_2f.
runtime_notes: Keep world layers on deterministic z-order bands: background < perch/floor < foreground bars < night overlay.
Register manifest execution_order in a startup resource so build order stays deterministic during content validation.
Read frame size and bounds from specs.dimensions_px rather than texture metadata during runtime checks.
